name: UN International Convention for the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearance (ICPED 2006)
summary: The UN International Convention for the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearance (ICPED) is a global supranational meta-framework enforced-disappearance binding-treaty instrument adopted by the UN General Assembly — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern universal multilateral binding-treaty framework operating specifically on enforced-disappearance framework (ICPED adopted 20 December 2006 by UN General Assembly Resolution 61/177 + effective 23 December 2010 — formal universal multilateral binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive enforced-disappearance framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-human-rights frameworks which operate on broader general-rights frameworks, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern universal binding-treaty framework operating on formal-enforced-disappearance-specific framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Article 25 children-victims-of-enforced-disappearance framework (ICPED Article 25 framework providing for state-party obligation to prevent + criminalise abduction of children whose parents are victims of enforced disappearance + abduction of children themselves subjected to enforced disappearance + falsification or destruction of children's identity documents + return of children with disappeared-parents framework — formal foundational children-victims-of-enforced-disappearance framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern universal binding-treaty framework operating formal children-victims-of-enforced-disappearance framework, with substantial relevance to PA framework via formal cross-border children-victims-of-parental-disappearance framework + formal children-with-disappeared-parents framework + 1976-1983 Argentinian-Dirty-War-stolen-children framework which catalysed ICPED development), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Article 24 victims-rights-to-the-truth framework (ICPED Article 24 framework providing 'Each victim has the right to know the truth regarding the circumstances of the enforced disappearance, the progress and results of the investigation and the fate of the disappeared person' — formal foundational victims-right-to-the-truth framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern universal binding-treaty framework operating formal victims-right-to-the-truth framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal Committee on Enforced Disappearances CED Committee framework with formal urgent-action procedure (CED Committee established under ICPED Article 26 + formal urgent-action procedure under ICPED Article 30 + formal individual communications framework under ICPED Article 31 + formal inter-state communications framework under ICPED Article 32 + formal visits framework under ICPED Article 33 — formal multi-procedure CED Committee framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern universal binding-treaty framework operating formal urgent-action procedure). The ICPED is binding upon 75 state parties as of 2026 — substantial state party + non-state-party framework reflecting growing but limited adoption framework. The Convention is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal Article 25 children-victims-of-enforced-disappearance + Article 24 victims-right-to-the-truth frameworks operate substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal cross-border children-victims-of-parental-disappearance framework + formal children-with-disappeared-parents framework. The Committee on Enforced Disappearances CED Committee is the apex monitoring framework body; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The ICPED is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.citation: UN International Convention for the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearance 2006
statutory_framework.title: ICPED
statutory_framework.relevance: UN International Convention for the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearance adopted 20 December 2006 by UN General Assembly Resolution 61/177 + effective 23 December 2010 — formal universal multilateral binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive enforced-disappearance framework.

statutory_framework.title: ICPED Article 25
statutory_framework.relevance: ICPED Article 25 framework providing for state-party obligation to prevent + criminalise abduction of children whose parents are victims of enforced disappearance + abduction of children themselves subjected to enforced disappearance + falsification or destruction of children's identity documents + return of children with disappeared-parents framework.

statutory_framework.title: ICPED Article 24
statutory_framework.relevance: ICPED Article 24 framework providing victims' right to know the truth regarding the circumstances of the enforced disappearance — formal foundational victims-right-to-the-truth framework.

statutory_framework.title: ICPED Articles 26-33
statutory_framework.relevance: ICPED Article 26 framework establishing Committee on Enforced Disappearances CED Committee + Article 30 urgent-action procedure + Article 31 individual communications + Article 32 inter-state communications + Article 33 visits framework.

statutory_framework.citation: UN Declaration on the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearance 1992
statutory_framework.title: UN Declaration 1992
statutory_framework.relevance: UN Declaration on the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearance adopted 18 December 1992 by UN General Assembly Resolution 47/133 — predecessor framework to ICPED.

apex_courts.name: Committee on Enforced Disappearances (CED Committee)
apex_courts.role: Apex monitoring framework body for ICPED with formal urgent-action procedure.

anonymisation_convention: ICPED-related individual communications are anonymised per CED Committee practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Argentinian Dirty War + stolen children framework
key_developments.description: Argentinian Dirty War 1976-1983 — pivotal historical framework catalysing ICPED development via formal stolen-children-of-disappeared-parents framework.

key_developments.title: UN Working Group on Enforced or Involuntary Disappearances
key_developments.description: UN Working Group on Enforced or Involuntary Disappearances WGEID established 29 February 1980 — formal UN Special Procedures framework on enforced-disappearance.

key_developments.title: UN Declaration on Enforced Disappearance
key_developments.description: UN Declaration on the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearance adopted 18 December 1992 by UN General Assembly Resolution 47/133 — predecessor framework to ICPED.

key_developments.title: ICPED adopted
key_developments.description: UN International Convention for the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearance adopted 20 December 2006 by UN General Assembly Resolution 61/177 — formal universal multilateral binding-treaty framework.

key_developments.title: ICPED enters force
key_developments.description: UN International Convention for the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearance entered force 23 December 2010 — formal universal multilateral binding-treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: Committee on Enforced Disappearances established
key_developments.description: Committee on Enforced Disappearances CED Committee established 31 May 2011 — formal CED Committee framework operational.

structural_findings: ICPED operates a supranational universal multilateral binding-treaty framework — places ICPED in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern universal multilateral binding-treaty framework operating specifically on enforced-disappearance framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal-enforced-disappearance-specific framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal Article 25 children-victims-of-enforced-disappearance framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern universal binding-treaty framework operating formal children-victims-of-enforced-disappearance framework with formal cross-border children-victims-of-parental-disappearance framework + children-with-disappeared-parents framework relevance.

Central jurisdiction of formal Article 24 victims-right-to-the-truth framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern universal binding-treaty framework operating formal victims-right-to-the-truth framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal Committee on Enforced Disappearances CED Committee framework with formal urgent-action procedure is structurally distinctive globally — only modern universal binding-treaty framework operating formal urgent-action procedure.
75 state party ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — substantial state party + non-state-party framework reflecting growing but limited adoption framework.
1976-1983 Argentinian Dirty War stolen-children-of-disappeared-parents framework catalysing ICPED development is structurally distinctive globally.
1980 WGEID + 1992 UN Declaration + 2006 ICPED + 2010 entry into force + 2011 CED Committee established enforced-disappearance-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: ICPED jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global enforced-disappearance universal multilateral binding-treaty meta-framework (ICPED 2006/2010 + Article 24 Victims-Right-to-the-Truth + Article 25 Children-Victims-of-Enforced-Disappearance + Articles 26-33 CED Committee Framework + UN Declaration 1992 + WGEID 1980 + Argentinian Dirty War 1976-1983 catalyst framework). Only modern universal multilateral binding-treaty framework operating specifically on enforced-disappearance framework globally + central jurisdiction of formal Article 25 children-victims-of-enforced-disappearance framework + central jurisdiction of formal Article 24 victims-right-to-the-truth framework + central jurisdiction of formal Committee on Enforced Disappearances CED Committee framework with formal urgent-action procedure + 75 state party ratification framework + 1976-1983 Argentinian Dirty War stolen-children-of-disappeared-parents framework catalysing ICPED development + 1980 WGEID + 1992 UN Declaration + 2006 ICPED + 2010 entry into force + 2011 CED Committee established enforced-disappearance-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: Article 25 children-victims-of-enforced-disappearance + Article 24 victims-right-to-the-truth frameworks operate substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal cross-border children-victims-of-parental-disappearance framework + formal children-with-disappeared-parents framework.

Joins global + supranational + universal-multilateral-enforced-disappearance-binding-treaty cluster + ICPED-only-universal-enforced-disappearance-binding-treaty-globally-distinctive + Article-25-Children-Victims-of-Enforced-Disappearance + Article-24-Victims-Right-to-the-Truth + Committee-on-Enforced-Disappearances-CED-Committee-Article-30-urgent-action-procedure + 75-state-party-ratification + Argentinian-Dirty-War-1976-1983-stolen-children-catalyst + UN-Working-Group-on-Enforced-or-Involuntary-Disappearances-WGEID-1980 + 1992-UN-Declaration-predecessor clusters within the corpus.
